title: Another Page
excerpt:
Overview
A second flat leaf page under Examples. Both this and Simple Page are children of
 because they share the same folder.
Siblings
Sibling pages appear at the same indentation level in the tree. Their order in the sidebar
follows the sort setting (default: A–Z by title).

title: Examples
excerpt:
Overview
This section contains example pages that demonstrate how the tree sidebar organises docs.
Pages placed directly inside a folder become children of the file named after that folder.
For example, and are siblings here because both live in
 and this file is named — matching the folder name.

body:
Overview
This section contains example pages that demonstrate how the tree sidebar organises docs.
Pages placed directly inside a folder become children of the file named after that folder.
For example, and are siblings here because both live in
 and this file is named — matching the folder name.
Key rule
A file named the same as its containing folder becomes the section root for that folder.
Every other file in that folder is a direct child of the root.
Subsections
Subfolder roots (e.g. ) appear as independent top-level nodes in the
tree — they are not children of this page. The tree only tracks one level of folder nesting
per section root.

title: Simple Page
excerpt:
Overview
This is a flat leaf page — it has no children and lives directly under the Examples section.
In the tree sidebar it appears as a plain link under Examples, with no expand toggle.
When to use flat pages
Use flat pages for standalone topics that don't need sub-pages — reference entries,
one-o
body:
Overview
This is a flat leaf page — it has no children and lives directly under the Examples section.
In the tree sidebar it appears as a plain link under Examples, with no expand toggle.
When to use flat pages
Use flat pages for standalone topics that don't need sub-pages — reference entries,
one-off guides, or short factual docs that won't grow into a section.

title: Child One
excerpt:

First child of the Subsection root. Lives at .
The tree sidebar shows this nested under Subsection with one level of indentation.

title: Subsection
excerpt:
Overview
This page is the root of the folder. It appears as an independent top-level
node in the tree sidebar — not as a child of Examples — because section roots are always
promoted to the top level.
Its children (, ) appear nested beneath it when expanded.
When to use nested sections
Use a subfold
body:
Overview
This page is the root of the folder. It appears as an independent top-level
node in the tree sidebar — not as a child of Examples — because section roots are always
promoted to the top level.
Its children (, ) appear nested beneath it when expanded.
When to use nested sections
Use a subfolder with a matching root file when a topic has enough sub-pages to warrant
grouping — typically three or more related pages that share a parent concept.
